ElementDescription: The NDAR Global Unique Identifier (GUID) for research subject
Subject ID how it's defined in lab/project
Date on which the interview/genetic test/sampling/imaging/biospecimen was completed. MM/DD/YYYY
Age in months at the time of the interview/test/sampling/imaging.
Notes: Age is rounded to chronological month. If the research participant is 15-days-old at time of interview, the appropriate value would be 0 months. If the participant is 16-days-old, the value would be 1 month.

ElementDescription: Sex of subject at birth
Notes: M = Male; F = Female; O=Other; NR = Not reported

ElementDescription: Timepoint/visit label
Notes: e.g., Baseline, 6-Month Follow Up, Year 2; Morning, At Bedtime

ElementDescription: Relationship to child
Notes: 1=Biological mother; 2=Stepmother; 3=Adoptive mother; 4=Foster mother; 5= Grandmother; 6=Biological father; 7=Stepfather; 8=Adoptive father; 9=Foster father; 10=Grandfather; 11=Other Legal Parent/Guardian; 12=Other (specify relationship)

ElementDescription: Specified other relationship to child
Child lives in respondent's home right now.
With whom does the child live, if not the respondent. Only completed if asi_residence_resp=0
Number of days per month that the residence sees child.
Vision problems [DISCONTINUE INTERVIEW IF THERE ARE SERIOUS PROBLEMS THAT ARE NOT CORRECTED WITH ASSISTIVE VISION TECHNOLOGY]
Hearing problems [DISCONTINUE INTERVIEW IF THERE ARE SERIOUS PROBLEMS THAT ARE NOT CORRECTED WITH ASSISTIVE VISION TECHNOLOGY]
No serious vision or hearing problems
Unknown if child has any serious vision or hearing problems
Child can walk on their own [MAKE SURE THAT RESPONDENT DOES NOT COUNT TEMPORARY DIFFICULTIES, SUCH AS A SPRAINED ANKLE, AS ''NOT WALKING'']. Discontinue interview if ''No''
Age in months when child first started walking without holding on.
Child currently receives any special educational services, such as speech therapy, a one-to-one aide, or time in a special education or resource classroom
Age in months when child first started using single words besides ''mama'' and ''dada'' to communicate.
Notes: 0::216=Age in months; 888=Not currently using single words

ElementDescription: Thinks that child's language is at least as good as other children his/her age.
Child uses at least 5 words on a daily basis.
Child puts words together to make phrases on a daily basis. Skipped if speech_5words=0
Child uses complex sentences on a daily basis. Skipped if speech_5words=0 OR speech_phrases=0
Example 1 of things child says during a typical day.
Example 2 of things child says during a typical day.
Interviewer rating: Child does not use single words yet OR uses 5 different words on a daily basis. [Do not administer later items designated ''VERBAL ONLY'']
Interviewer rating: Child uses at least 5 different single words on a daily basis but does not use phrases on a daily basis. [Do not administer later items designated ''VERBAL ONLY'']
Interviewer rating: Child uses complex sentences on a daily basis. (Do not administer later items designated ''NONVERBAL ONLY'')
Modality for administration/recording of the behavior section of the survey.
Notes: 1=No reading (Respondent wants to write down choices); 2=Yes reading (respondent wants questions to read)

ElementDescription: How often does child make eye contact when talking with respondent?
If you call child's name, how often does he/she respond by looking at you?
How often does child use his/her language for social purposes, such as chatting or making comments just to be friendly? [VERBAL ONLY]
Notes: 1=Not at all; 2=Occasionally; 3=Often; 4=Very frequently; 5=NOT APPLICABLE (e.g., child is nonverbal)

ElementDescription: When you ask child a question, how often does he/she answer you? [VERBAL ONLY]
Notes: 1=Not at all; 2=Occasionally; 3=Often; 4=Very frequently; 5=NOT APPLICABLE (e.g., child is nonverbal)

ElementDescription: IF you say something to child without asking a direct question, how often does he/she respond to what you said? [VERBAL ONLY]
Notes: 1=Not at all; 2=Occasionally; 3=Often; 4=Very frequently; 5=NOT APPLICABLE (e.g., child is nonverbal)

ElementDescription: How often does child start a conversation with you? [VERBAL ONLY]
Notes: 1=Not at all; 2=Occasionally; 3=Often; 4=Very frequently; 5=NOT APPLICABLE (e.g., child is nonverbal)

ElementDescription: How often does child ask you questions about your interests or experiences? [VERBAL ONLY]
Notes: 1=Not at all; 2=Occasionally; 3=Often; 4=Very frequently; 5=NOT APPLICABLE (e.g., child is nonverbal)

ElementDescription: How often does child talk about a particular topic over and over to an unusual degree? [VERBAL ONLY]
Notes: 1=Not at all; 2=Occasionally; 3=Often; 4=Very frequently; 5=NOT APPLICABLE (e.g., child is nonverbal)

ElementDescription: How often does child say things that other people think are unusual or strange? [VERBAL ONLY]
Notes: 1=Not at all; 2=Occasionally; 3=Often; 4=Very frequently; 5=NOT APPLICABLE (e.g., child is nonverbal)

ElementDescription: Some children sound robotic or too flat when they talk. Others sound different because their voices are consistently too high pitched or sing-song like. How often does child sound different than other kids his/her age? [VERBAL ONLY]
Notes: 1=Not at all; 2=Occasionally; 3=Often; 4=Very frequently; 5=NOT APPLICABLE (e.g., child is nonverbal)

ElementDescription: How often does child use his/her finger to point out something of interest?
How often does child use simple gestures, such as waving good-bye or giving a ''thumbs up'', without being told to do so?
How often does he/she nod his/her head to mean yes without being told to do so?
If child is not smiling already, and you smile at him/her without touching him/her, how often does he/she smile back at you?
How often does child show facial expressions that do not match the situation?
How often does child offer to share food or other things with people, without being asked or told to share?
When someone else is hurt or sick, how often does child notice that something is wrong?
When an adult outside of the family talks to him/her, how often does he/she respond appropriately, such as by smiling, saying ''Hi'', or talking to the person?
On the other hand, how often does child respond inappropriately to an adult outside of the family, such as by ignoring the person?
In social situations, such as eating at a restaurant or visiting someone's home, how often does child show behaviors that are NOT appropriate to that situation?
Does child prefer to spend time by him/herself? How often?
When child is around other children his/her age, how often does he/she approach them or try to join in?
If another child tries to interact with child, how often does he/she respond positively and do something to try to keep the interaction going?
When groups of children are playing game, such as tag or basketball, how often does child join in on his/her own and play?
How often does child play pretend with other children, such as playing house or school? [ONLY CHILDREN UNDER 10]
Notes: 1=Not at all; 2=Occasionally; 3=Often; 4=Very frequently; 5=NOT APPLICABLE (e.g., child is 10 or older); 6=Unknown (e.g., respondent insists that he/she has not observed interactions with other children)

ElementDescription: How often does child insist on playing in a very particular way? [ONLY CHILDREN UNDER 10]
Notes: 1=Not at all; 2=Occasionally; 3=Often; 4=Very frequently; 5=NOT APPLICABLE (e.g., child is 10 or older)

ElementDescription: How often does child make plans or verbally express an interest in getting together with other kids outside of school?
Notes: 1=Not at all; 2=Occasionally; 3=Often; 4=Very frequently; 5=NOT APPLICABLE (e.g., child is nonverbal)

ElementDescription: How often does child seem socially awkward?
Notes: 1=Not at all; 2=Occasionally; 3=Often; 4=Very frequently; 5=NOT APPLICABLE (e.g., child is nonverbal)

ElementDescription: How often does child have difficulty maintaining friendships or keeping friends after he/she makes them?
Notes: 1=Not at all; 2=Occasionally; 3=Often; 4=Very frequently; 5=NOT APPLICABLE (e.g., child is nonverbal); 6=Unknown (e.g., respondent insists that he/she does not know)

ElementDescription: During free time, how often does child seem to get stuck on doing the same activity for a long period of time?
How often does child focus his/her attention on specific parts of toys or objects rather than using them as they were intended?
Some children are overly sensitive to loud noises or are upset by certain sounds, sights, tastes, or feels of things. Is child bothered by any of these things? How often?
How often does child show sensory interests?

Some children have strong interests in unusual things, such as elevators, spoons, or people's home addresses, or they might be interested in a specific aspect of something, such as the letter ''H'' of the alphabet. Does child have any unusual interests like this?
How often does child do or say things related to this unusual interest? [IF asi_behavior_preoccupy=1 AND child is verbal]
Notes: 1=Not at all; 2=Occasionally; 3=Often; 4=Very frequently; 5=NOT APPLICABLE (i.e., respondent answered ''No'' above)

ElementDescription: How often does child do or say things related to this unusual interest? [IF asi_behavior_preoccupy=1 AND child is nonverbal]
Notes: 1=Not at all; 2=Occasionally; 3=Often; 4=Very frequently; 5=NOT APPLICABLE (i.e., respondent answered ''No'' above)

ElementDescription: Does child have any overly intense interests or intense hobbies, such as in trains or geography or other special interests that take up a large amount of his/her time?
Specify intense interest. [IF asi_behavior_interest=1]
How often does child spend time talking about or doing things related to this intense interest [IF asi_behavior_interest=1 AND child is verbal]
Notes: 1=Not at all; 2=Occasionally; 3=Often; 4=Very frequently; 5=NOT APPLICABLE (i.e., respondent answered ''No'' above)

ElementDescription: How often does child spend time talking about or doing things related to this intense interest [IF asi_behavior_interest=1 AND child is nonverbal]
Notes: 1=Not at all; 2=Occasionally; 3=Often; 4=Very frequently; 5=NOT APPLICABLE (i.e., respondent answered ''No'' above)

ElementDescription: Some children show repetitive hand movements, such as flicking their fingers or flapping their hands. How often does child move his/her hands or fingers in these ways?
Some children show unusual repetitive whole body movements, such as hopping while waving their arms. How often does child move his/her body in these ways?
Some children are bothered by minor changes in their daily routine. How often does child react negatively to changes like this in his/her day?
How often does child insist on doing things in a very particular way or order?
Have you ever had concerns about child's behavior or development?
What concerns you the most right now about child's behavior or development?
How old was child when you first wondered if there might be something different or not quite right about his/her behavior or development? Age in months.
Did child lose any language or social skills during the first few years of life after he/she first gained them?
What type of skills did he/she lose? [If asi_diag_loss=1]
How old was he/she when he/she lost those skills? Age in months. [If asi_diag_loss=1]
Has a medical, mental health, or school professional ever expressed concerns that child might have an autism spectrum disorder (ASD), like autism, Asperger syndrome, or PDD-NOS?
How concerned are you that child might have an autism spectrum disorder (ASD)?
Notes: 1=Not at all concerned; 2=A little concerned; 3=Pretty concerned; 4=Extremely concerned; 5=NA (Parent indicates that child previously received an ASD diagnosis from a professional)

ElementDescription: Interview conducted over phone or in-person
Respondent required response options read to them for the majority of the interview
Total time of interview in minutes
Notes and impressions (any behavioral or circumstances occurrences to flag)
Specify reason for flagging interview (if asi_interview_flag=1)
Is there anything important about child's behavior or development that I should have asked you? (Optional question)
Do you have any other concerns or questions about today's interview or about your upcoming appointments? (Optional question)
